title: sanguinaria canadensis var. rotundifolia
intro: Sanguinaria canadensis var. rotundifolia, also known as yellow adder root or round-leaved bloodroot, is a perennial plant that belongs to the poppy family (Papaveraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties, particularly the isoquinoline alkaloids present in its roots. The medicinal properties of Sanguinaria canadensis var. rotundifolia have been associated with various health benefits, including its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to inhibit the growth of cancer cells. In herbal preparations, the roots of the plant are commonly used to make teas, tinctures, and capsules, although its use should be carefully monitored due to potential side effects and interactions with other medications.
